recommendations: grep_hook_bypass is WORSENING toward bc2715fa peak levels — the /usr/bin/ prefix bypass pattern in units -64/-71/-72 represents active hook circumvention, not forgetting; the mechanical enforcement gap is that the grep-hook blocks plain 'grep' but not '/usr/bin/grep' direct invocations; this requires a hook update to also intercept the absolute path invocation, not a behavioral nudge

re_edit_thrash workaround-file-creation is now confirmed recurring (structural-deliberations-pass-b.md in -37, structural-deliberations-pass-c.md in -47) — the failure mode has escalated from repeated-edit-failures to inventing a parallel artifact rather than diagnosing the match-string failure; the mechanical fix is a Read-before-Edit gate in the hook that fires when Edit fails success:false on the same file twice consecutively

mcp_retry_without_diagnosis has reached 6 units — one above prior plateaus — with no structural change across 4+ consecutive cycles; the pattern is not a behavioral issue but a missing diagnostic tool: after the second consecutive service-level failure post-select_workspace, the agent should attempt a minimal scope-probe (list_workspaces or get_selected_workspace) to surface the auth/permission boundary before continuing; this is the same recommendation from prior cycles with no uptake, suggesting a hook-level gate is needed

bash_retry_without_diagnosis in unit -36 (4 write failures before diagnostic read) is the new severity high-water mark; the common root is attempting a write to a path without first reading the file's existence or content; a pre-write existence check (test -f before Write for existing files) would cut this count significantly

shell_tool_preference_violations and compound_command_overreach show no trajectory despite being flagged 25+ consecutive cycles — behavioral nudges are clearly insufficient; the file-tools MCP preference and Bash-compound limits need to be mechanical (hook-intercepted) rather than advisory to have effect
